# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Upload Internal Distribution Artifacts

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version:
        description: 'Application Version'
        required: true
        default: '1.0.0'
      environment:
        description: 'Build Environment (e.g., dev, staging, prod)'
        required: true
        default: 'dev'

jobs:
  build-and-upload-artifacts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up build environment (e.g., Node.js, .NET, Go)
      # Replace with actual setup steps for your application
      # Example for Node.js:
      # uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      # with:
      #   node-version: '20'
      # Example for .NET:
      # uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
      # with:
      #   dotnet-version: '8.0.x'
      run: |
        echo "Setting up build environment..."
        # Simulate environment setup
        sleep 2

    - name: Build application
      # Replace with actual build commands for your application
      run: |
        echo "Building application for version ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} for environment ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}..."
        # Simulate compilation of binaries
        mkdir -p ./dist/bin
        echo "Simulated binary content for MyApp-linux-x64-v${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" > ./dist/bin/MyApp-linux-x64
        echo "Simulated binary content for MyApp-windows-x64-v${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.exe" > ./dist/bin/MyApp-windows-x64.exe

        # Simulate creation of configuration files
        mkdir -p ./dist/config
        echo "{ \"version\": \"${{ github.event.inputs.version }}\", \"env\": \"${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}\", \"setting\": \"value\" }" > ./dist/config/appsettings.${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.json
        echo "log4j.properties=content" > ./dist/config/log4j.properties

        # Simulate creation of deployment scripts
        mkdir -p ./dist/scripts
        echo "#!/bin/bash\necho 'Deploying MyApp v${{ github.event.inputs.version }} to ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}'" > ./dist/scripts/deploy.sh
        chmod +x ./dist/scripts/deploy.sh
        echo "powershell -Command \"Write-Host 'Deploying MyApp v${{ github.event.inputs.version }} to ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}'\"" > ./dist/scripts/deploy.ps1

        echo "Build completed."

    - name: Upload build artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: application-artifacts-${{ github.event.inputs.version }}-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}
        path: |
          ./dist/bin/
          ./dist/config/
          ./dist/scripts/
        retention-days: 7 # Keep artifacts for 7 days. Adjust as needed for your retention policy.
        if-no-files-found: error # Fail the workflow if no files are found to upload
      env:
        RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        BUILD_VERSION: ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}
        BUILD_ENV: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}

    - name: Print artifact download instructions
      run: |
        echo "Artifacts 'application-artifacts-${{ github.event.inputs.version }}-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}' have been uploaded."
        echo "You can download them from the 'Summary' page of this workflow run, under the 'Artifacts' section."
        echo "Direct link (if available): https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
        echo "The artifacts include compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts."
